title: thymus pulegioides var. adscendens
intro: Thymus pulegioides var. adscendens, also known as creeping thyme, is a subspecies of low-growing, fragrant herb that belongs to the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Creeping thyme can be prepared into teas, infusions, essential oils, and topical creams, allowing for versatile use in both medicinal and culinary applications.
